findings.title: Disabled URMA failover still resets atomics on every hot-path sample
findings.impact: When `urma_failover_success_rate_ratio` is set to 0.0 to disable failover, every URMA Set/Get result still enters `RecordUrmaResult` and calls `ResetWindow()`, issuing several atomic stores per foreground request after the caller has already taken a timestamp. In high-throughput clients this defeats the expected low-overhead disabled mode and adds unnecessary cache-line traffic on the request path.
findings.fix: Make the disabled state a cheap fast path: avoid per-request resets after the state is already clear, or gate tracking before taking the timestamp/calling `RecordUrmaResult`; clear the window once when the config transitions to disabled instead of resetting on every sample.
